name: Storing Canned Foods
recipeIngredient: Lid cleaning solution or mild soap and water
Jar rinsing solution or clean, warm water
Clean, dry towel or paper towels for drying
recipeInstructions:
1. If the jar lids are tightly vacuum sealed after cooling, remove the screw bands.
2. Wash the lid under running water using a cleaning solution (or mild soap) to remove any food residue.
3. Rinse and thoroughly dry the jar.
4. Label each jar with an identifying name and date before storing it in a clean, cool, dark, dry place.
5. Do not store jars above 95°F (35°C) or near sources of heat such as hot pipes, ovens, furnaces, uninsulated attics, or direct sunlight.
6. Avoid storing jars where they may freeze, as this can soften the food contents and potentially lead to spoilage if recontamination occurs.
Note: Freezing canned foods will not cause spoilage unless the jars become unsealed and contaminated. If storage in freezing conditions is necessary, wrap the jars in newspapers, place them in heavy-duty containers or boxes for added protection, and cover with additional newspaper and blankets.
